findings.statement: When SPY daily closes within 1% above the 200-day SMA, SPY closes higher 10 trading days later at a rate above 65% — tested over 5 years (Jan 2019–Dec 2023), 52 occurrences, 73% positive.
findings.verdict_reason: 73% of 52 occurrences above the 65% holds threshold

validations.tradingview_query: SPY 1D close within 1% above SMA(200) → 10-bar forward return
validations.data_summary: SPY 1D: 52 occurrences Jan 2019–Dec 2023; 38/52 (73.1%) closed higher 10 trading days later
validations.result: 38 of 52 occurrences (73.1%) resulted in SPY closing higher 10 trading days after the trigger; 14 did not
caveats: 5-year lookback (2019–2023) spans a predominantly bull-market period — base rate of SPY closing higher over any 10-day window is ~55% in this window
73% vs ~55% base rate = ~18pp lift; statistically meaningful at N=52 but would strengthen with a longer or out-of-sample lookback
occurrences near the start of strong trends may be clustered — each close is counted independently
2020 COVID crash briefly broke the 200 SMA — those breaches are not included (trigger requires close above)
